title: Add env multi-select filter to dashboard
description:
## Context
Per SPEC.md's Phase 1 filter requirement: date range + env multi-select. The days
filter exists via ?days=7|30|90; env filter does not. With three writers soon
(clear-pipeline prod, staging, Nikita's local-nikita), each person needs to
focus on their own rows.

## Files to touch
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — every aggregate function takes an
  optional `envs: string[]` param and appends `AND pr.env = ANY(${envs})` (or the
  llm_call-side equivalent) when non-empty. Add `fetchAvailableEnvs()` returning
  all distinct `pipeline_run.env` values.
- [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx) — read `searchParams.env` (may be string |
  string[]), normalise to string[], pass to each query. Render a horizontal list
  of checkbox-links: each toggles one env in/out of the URL.

## UI sketch
```
Filter: [✓] prod  [✓] staging  [ ] local-nikita  [clear]
```
Each checkbox is an `<a>` that links to the same page with the env list updated
(add if not present, remove if present). Server-rendered, no JS.

## Testing
- Local smoke: insert runs across envs, confirm filters narrow each section.
- Edge case: selected env with zero calls in the window — each section shows
  its "No data"/"No runs" state.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- Stage filter (future — start with env since it's the team's primary axis).
- Date-range picker beyond the existing 7d/30d/90d links.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard URL accepts repeated ?env= query params (e.g. ?env=prod&env=staging). All five aggregates (hero, by-env chart, by-stage chart, top runs, models seen) are filtered. UI shows checkboxes for each distinct env discovered in the DB. Clicking a checkbox navigates to a new URL (server-side filtering, no client state). Empty selection = all envs (current behaviour).

comments.author: positonic
comments.text: Implemented in d785841. Added fetchAvailableEnvs() (DISTINCT pr.env). Every aggregate fn gains optional envs[] and joins pipeline_run; applied to hero, by-env, by-stage, top-runs, models-seen AND to the parse-error/latency/cache tiles added by 2id/7xm/82h so filtering is consistent across the whole page. Days nav links preserve env params. Env chip strip is server-rendered <a> toggles with a 'clear' link. NOTE: dropped `= ANY(${envs})` in favour of `IN (${sql.join(...)})` because drizzle's sql template spreads JS arrays into separate parameters, so the ANY-array form errored with 'malformed array literal' when postgres-js flattened the array to the first element. Verified: ?env=prod narrows to claude-sonnet-4-6 only; ?env=local-nikita narrows to nikita-v1; ?env=prod&env=staging combines both; unknown env yields empty states; no env param = all envs unchanged. lint + build pass.

title: Add parse-error-rate tile to dashboard
description:
## Context
The `llm_call.parse_error` column is already populated by `POST /api/calls` whenever
the pipeline fails to parse Claude's response, but nothing surfaces it in the UI.
This is the fastest signal that a prompt is drifting — a spike means a stage is
producing output that no longer parses. Should be a prominent tile next to the
24h hero stat so it's the first thing the team sees on a bad day.

## Files to touch
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — add `fetchParseErrorRate(days)` that
  returns `{stage, errors, total, rate}[]` grouped by stage. Include only rows where
  `created_at >= now() - $days * interval '1 day'`.
- [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx) — render a new section between the hero tile
  and the stacked-bar grid. One row per stage with: stage name, error count /
  total count, percentage. Red text when rate > 1%. Grey '—' row when no calls.

## SQL sketch
```sql
SELECT
  stage,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE parse_error IS NOT NULL) AS errors,
  COUNT(*) AS total
FROM llm_call
WHERE created_at >= now() - ($1::int * interval '1 day')
GROUP BY stage
ORDER BY stage;
```

## Testing
- Insert a few rows locally via `docker exec insights-pg psql ...` with
  `parse_error` set and unset, refresh http://localhost:3001, confirm percentages
  match.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- Historical chart of error rate over time (future phase).
- Drill-down to individual failing calls (Phase 3 call browser).
acceptance_criteria: A new tile on / shows the parse error rate for the selected window (count(parse_error IS NOT NULL) / count(*)) broken down by stage. Shows '—' when no calls in range. Visible screenshots of the tile with and without errors.

comments.author: positonic
comments.text: Implemented in eefd35c. Added fetchParseErrorRate() in src/lib/queries.ts and a table section on src/app/page.tsx (between hero and stacked-bar grid) showing errors/total/rate per stage. Red when rate > 1%. Verified locally with seeded rows: classify 3/4=75% rendered red; assess/group 0% not red. Empty state shows '—'. lint + build pass.

title: Add p50/p95 latency by stage to dashboard
description:
## Context
`llm_call.latency_ms` is captured per call but never shown. p50/p95 per stage is
the standard operational metric for spotting model regressions, Anthropic
slowdowns, or prompt bloat. Pairs well with the parse-error-rate tile.

## Files to touch
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — add `fetchLatencyByStage(days)`
  returning `{stage, calls, p50, p95, p99}[]`.
- [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx) — new table section below the stacked
  bar charts, before "Top 20 runs". Columns: STAGE, CALLS, p50 ms, p95 ms, p99 ms.
  Red when p95 > 5000ms.

## SQL sketch
```sql
SELECT
  stage,
  COUNT(*) AS calls,
  percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY latency_ms)::int AS p50,
  percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY latency_ms)::int AS p95,
  percentile_cont(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY latency_ms)::int AS p99
FROM llm_call
WHERE latency_ms IS NOT NULL
  AND created_at >= now() - ($1::int * interval '1 day')
GROUP BY stage
ORDER BY stage;
```

## Testing
- Insert ~20 rows per stage locally with varying `latency_ms` values, confirm the
  percentiles match hand-checked values.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- Latency histograms (future).
- Latency by model (future — current signal is stage-level).
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows a table with one row per stage: calls, p50_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms. Values formatted with thousand separators. Percentile computation uses PostgreSQL's percentile_cont aggregate. Verified with seeded rows.

comments.author: positonic
comments.text: Implemented in c9287bc. Added fetchLatencyByStage() in src/lib/queries.ts using percentile_cont(0.5/0.95/0.99). New table section on src/app/page.tsx between the bar grid and top-runs. Red p95 when > 5000ms. Verified with 20 seeded rows per stage: classify p50=1110 p95=1200 p99=1714 (hand-checked matches linear interpolation), group p95=6950 rendered red. Values comma-formatted via toLocaleString. lint + build pass.

title: Add cache hit rate + cache savings tile
description:
## Context
Prompt caching is a big cost lever for the pipeline (system prompts are large and
repeated). We already store `cache_read_tokens` and `cache_create_tokens` but
surface nothing. A hit ratio tile tells the team whether caching is working, and
a "dollars saved" estimate makes the impact legible.

## Files to touch
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — add `fetchCacheStats(days)`
  returning `{model, input, cache_read, cache_create}[]` grouped by model.
- [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx) — new tile next to the hero stat showing
  hit ratio (%) and estimated savings (USD). Compute savings server-side using
  [src/lib/prices.ts](src/lib/prices.ts) — for each priced model, savings =
  cache_read_tokens * (full_input_price - cache_read_price) / 1e6. Skip models
  missing from MODEL_PRICES.

## SQL sketch
```sql
SELECT
  model,
  SUM(input_tokens) AS input,
  SUM(cache_read_tokens) AS cache_read,
  SUM(cache_create_tokens) AS cache_create
FROM llm_call
WHERE created_at >= now() - ($1::int * interval '1 day')
GROUP BY model;
```

## Testing
- Insert rows locally with and without cache_read populated; confirm ratio and
  savings math.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- Cache hit rate trend over time.
- Per-prompt cache analysis.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows a 'Prompt caching' tile with: cache hit ratio (cache_read / (input_tokens + cache_read)), total cache_read_tokens, total cache_create_tokens, estimated dollars saved by caching (cache_read_tokens * model's full input price - cache_read_tokens * model's cacheRead price, summed across models present in MODEL_PRICES). Null-model rows excluded from savings calc.

comments.author: positonic
comments.text: Implemented in 1134137. fetchCacheStats() groups SUMs by model; summarizeCache() on the page computes hit ratio and savings using MODEL_PRICES (models missing from the map are excluded from the savings calc but still counted in total read/create). New tile sits beside the 24h hero in a 2-col grid. Verified with 50000 cache_read on claude-sonnet-4-6 + existing 100900 input: hit=33.1%, savings=$0.135 (hand-check: 50000*(3-0.3)/1e6=0.135). lint + build pass.

title: Spike: decide token strategy (one shared vs per-writer)
description:
## Context
Currently one shared `INSIGHTS_INGEST_TOKEN` authenticates every POST. This is
fine for 1–2 trusted writers. As soon as Nikita's classifier joins, three people
know the token (James, Prajava, Nikita). At four or five writers, a leak forces
rotating every consumer.

Options:

- **(A) Keep shared token.** Simplest. Rotation = update Vercel + DM new value
  to each writer. Any leak = rotate everyone.
- **(B) Per-writer tokens server-side.** `INSIGHTS_INGEST_TOKENS` becomes a
  JSON map like `{"clear-pipeline-prod":"hex...", "nikita":"hex..."}`. Auth
  middleware looks up the token and stores the matched writer name in a
  (new, optional) `llm_call.written_by` column. Leak = rotate just one.

## Tradeoffs
- B is a half-day change (add a column, tweak auth, teach writers to use their
  own token).
- B gives post-hoc attribution for noisy writers.
- A is still defensible if the team stays small.

## What this spike produces
A decision entry in [SPEC.md](SPEC.md)'s Decisions section, plus either:
- If A: close this ticket, no code.
- If B: open a follow-up implementation ticket with the column addition +
  auth.ts change.

## Research steps
1. Confirm how many writers are realistic in the next 3 months (ask James).
2. Look at whether Vercel env vars can hold a JSON map cleanly (they can — it's
   just a string).
3. Consider if attribution (knowing who wrote which row) has value beyond auth.

## Out of scope
- Implementing option B in this ticket — that's a separate ticket if chosen.
- OAuth, API keys per user, signed requests — all overkill for Phase 1.
acceptance_criteria: A short written recommendation (added to SPEC.md under 'Decisions') picking one of: (A) keep one shared INSIGHTS_INGEST_TOKEN, (B) move to a map of named tokens server-side. Covers: rotation cost, leak blast radius, how many writers trigger the switch, whether to log which token ingested each row. No code required unless the decision is 'switch now'.

title: Schedule 20-min schema review call with Nikita before he codes his classifier's insights writer
description:
## Context
SPEC.md §9 Open Question 2: 'Should Nikita be looped in on the schema? Lean:
yes, 20-min call before we mark schema stable.' Now is exactly that moment —
schema is deployed, Nikita hasn't started his Python insights client yet, so
changes are still cheap. Every writer we add after this compounds schema-change
coordination cost.

## Why this is P1
This is time-sensitive in a way the dashboard tickets aren't. Every day Nikita
writes code against his own mental model of the schema is a day of rework risk.

## Agenda to share in advance
1. **Env / model naming.** His classifier should use `env='local-nikita'` (or
   agree a different value) and `model='nikita-v1'` so MODEL_PRICES can be
   pre-populated with zero-price entries and his rows don't flag red in 'Models
   seen'.
2. **`signal_id` convention.** The single most important agreement: Nikita's
   `signal_id` MUST equal clear-pipeline's `signal_id` for the same underlying
   signal. This is the Phase 4 run-comparison join key. If he invents his own
   ids, we lose the Claude-vs-Nikita comparison forever without backfill.
3. **Inference shape.** Does his classifier do one-row-per-inference or does it
   batch? Batching means `input_tokens`/`latency_ms` have different meanings and
   might warrant a `batch_size` column. Decide: support batching now (additive
   column) or enforce one-call-per-inference.
4. **Test signal overlap.** Does his eval set overlap with clear-pipeline's
   inputs? Phase 4 comparison needs shared signals.
5. **Token strategy.** Heads-up that we might move from one shared token to
   per-writer tokens (see separate spike ticket) — is he OK being the second
   user of the shared token short-term?

## Deliverable
Add a short `## Decisions` section at the bottom of [SPEC.md](SPEC.md) capturing
each of the five answers with a date stamp, e.g.:
```
## Decisions
- 2026-05-XX — Nikita's classifier writes as env='local-nikita', model='nikita-v1'.
- 2026-05-XX — signal_id uses the same value as clear-pipeline; Nikita will pull
  signal fixtures from <location>.
- ...
```

## Out of scope
- Actually implementing his insights client (that's Nikita's work in his repo).
- Schema changes, unless the call surfaces something critical.
acceptance_criteria: Calendar invite sent. Agenda shared in advance covering: (1) env/model string conventions for his classifier, (2) signal_id must match clear-pipeline's value for the same signal (join key for Phase 4), (3) whether his classifier emits one row per inference or batches (schema implication), (4) whether he wants to share signal fixtures. Meeting happens. Decisions captured in a short note added to SPEC.md under a new 'Decisions' section.

title: Add /live page with last-60-min view and auto-refresh
description:
## Context
When debugging a live pipeline problem, the 30-day aggregates are useless.
Operators need a short-window view that reflects the pipeline as it runs right
now. A separate route keeps the main dashboard un-cluttered.

## Files to touch
- New file `src/app/live/page.tsx` — same structure as
  [src/app/page.tsx](src/app/page.tsx), but passes a minutes-based window to
  queries instead of days. `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";` and add
  `<meta httpEquiv="refresh" content="30" />` in the page's JSX.
- [src/lib/queries.ts](src/lib/queries.ts) — add a minutes-based variant of each
  aggregate (or refactor existing ones to take `{windowSeconds: number}`). Keep
  the DB filter as `created_at >= now() - interval '$1 seconds'`.

## Design notes
- Meta refresh is the simplest option; no client JS. Next.js with
  `force-dynamic` re-renders on every request, so a 30s refresh re-queries the
  DB every 30s.
- In the hero, "last 24h / prior 24h" becomes "last 60 min / prior 60 min".

## Testing
- Local smoke: insert fresh rows, open /live, wait 30s, confirm the numbers update.
- `npm run build` must pass.

## Out of scope
- WebSocket / SSE streaming — meta refresh is sufficient for Phase 1.
- Tail of most-recent individual calls (that's Phase 3 call browser).
acceptance_criteria: New route /live shows the same sections as / but windowed to the last 60 minutes instead of days. Page refreshes every 30 seconds (meta refresh or server-triggered revalidation). Clearly labelled 'Live — last 60 minutes'. Links back to / at top.
